boj_gradual_hiking_cycle.claim: The Bank of Japan is in a gradual tightening cycle, having raised rates from negative territory to 0.75% since March 2024, with a cautious pace dependent on data and inflation outlook.

provenance.definition: The Bank of Japan is in a gradual tightening cycle, having raised rates from negative territory to 0.75% since March 2024, with a cautious pace dependent on data and inflation outlook.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: more specific levels held 0 case(s) (< 4); pooled up to 'same_institution'

cases.description: BOJ raised policy rate from -0.1% to 0-0.1% in March 2024, first hike in 17 years.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank decided to change the guideline for market operations... the uncollateralized overnight call rate to be around 0 to 0.1%.

cases.description: BOJ raised rate to 0.25% in July 2024.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.25%.

cases.description: BOJ raised rate to 0.5% in January 2025.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.5%.

cases.description: BOJ raised rate to 0.75% in March 2025.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.75%.

dissenting_votes_on_hold.claim: At the April 2026 meeting, three board members dissented in favor of a hike to 1.0%, indicating internal pressure for tightening.

provenance.definition: At the April 2026 meeting, three board members dissented in favor of a hike to 1.0%, indicating internal pressure for tightening.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: April 27-28, 2026 meeting: three members proposed raising rate to 1.0%, defeated 6-3.
cases.basis_quote: Three members proposed raising the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 1.0%... the proposal was defeated by a majority vote.

market_expectations_elevated.claim: Market-implied probability of a June hike is around 63% as of mid-May 2026, with Reuters reporting the BOJ has signaled a possible hike.

provenance.definition: Market-implied probability of a June hike is around 63% as of mid-May 2026, with Reuters reporting the BOJ has signaled a possible hike.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Reuters reported on May 12, 2026 that the BOJ has 'locked in' signals for a June rate hike.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan has locked in signals for a June rate hike, sources say.

nakagawa_junko.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold in favor of a hike

provenance.definition: Board member Nakagawa Junko votes to raise the policy rate when the majority votes to hold.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any Monetary Policy Meeting where Nakagawa Junko is a voting member and the final decision is to hold the rate, and she votes for a hike.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Meetings where she votes with the majority or abstains.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: April 27-28, 2026 meeting: Nakagawa Junko proposed raising rate to 1.0%, defeated.
cases.basis_quote: Three members proposed raising the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 1.0%... the proposal was defeated by a majority vote.

excluded.reason: quote not verifiable in the permitted evidence; action vague/untyped — cannot map to an action class

takata_hajime.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold in favor of a hike

provenance.definition: Board member Takata Hajime votes to raise the policy rate when the majority votes to hold.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any Monetary Policy Meeting where Takata Hajime is a voting member and the final decision is to hold the rate, and he votes for a hike.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Meetings where he votes with the majority or abstains.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: April 27-28, 2026 meeting: Takata Hajime proposed raising rate to 1.0%, defeated.
cases.basis_quote: Three members proposed raising the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 1.0%... the proposal was defeated by a majority vote.

excluded.reason: quote not verifiable in the permitted evidence; action vague/untyped — cannot map to an action class

tamura_naoki.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold in favor of a hike

provenance.definition: Board member Tamura Naoki votes to raise the policy rate when the majority votes to hold.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any Monetary Policy Meeting where Tamura Naoki is a voting member and the final decision is to hold the rate, and he votes for a hike.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Meetings where he votes with the majority or abstains.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: April 27-28, 2026 meeting: Tamura Naoki proposed raising rate to 1.0%, defeated.
cases.basis_quote: Three members proposed raising the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 1.0%... the proposal was defeated by a majority vote.

excluded.reason: quote not verifiable in the permitted evidence; action vague/untyped — cannot map to an action class

outcome_reference_class.quantity: BOJ raises short-term policy interest rate at June 15-16, 2026 meeting

provenance.definition: The Bank of Japan announces a guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate higher than the current 0.75% (i.e., at least 1.0%) in the official Statement on Monetary Policy following the June 15-16, 2026 meeting.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any increase in the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate above 0.75% as stated in the official Statement.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: No change or a decrease in the rate; meeting cancellation.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: more specific levels held 0 case(s) (< 4); pooled up to 'same_institution'

cases.description: March 19, 2024: BOJ raised rate from -0.1% to 0-0.1%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank decided to change the guideline for market operations... the uncollateralized overnight call rate to be around 0 to 0.1%.
The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.25%.
The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.5%.
The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.75%.

boj_policy_board.consequence_of_nonparticipation: Non-participating members are recorded as absent; their vote is not counted; quorum must still be met.
